overview_paragraph: Kraków shines in late spring (May–June) and early autumn (September–October), when the Planty ring is in full leaf or turning gold, café tables spill onto the Rynek Główny, and the riverside boulevards beneath Wawel come alive. Summer (July–August) brings warm evenings, festivals in Kazimierz, and the year's biggest crowds; winter (December–February) is cold and often snowy but deeply atmospheric—Christmas markets on Europe's largest medieval square, candlelit churches, and steaming milk-bar comfort food. March and April warm slowly around Easter traditions and early-music concerts.
January: Snow on the Sukiennice, mulled wine lingering after the markets, and fairy-tale szopki cribs on display.
Frosty Rynek mornings, warm milk-bar żurek, and Wawel's courtyards hushed and crowd-free.
February: Fat Thursday pączki by the dozen, cosy cellar restaurants, and snowy day trips to Zakopane's slopes.
Candlelit Gothic churches, steaming barszcz, and the quiet beauty of a winter Old Town.
March: First green in the Planty, Easter markets returning to the square, and early-music concerts in old churches.
Brisk riverside walks below Wawel, hand-painted pisanki eggs, and the slow thaw of spring.
April: Easter markets on the Rynek, Rękawka bonfires at Krakus Mound, and blossoms ringing the Old Town.
Misteria Paschalia in candlelit naves, palm bouquets, and longer light over the Vistula boulevards.
May: Juwenalia student parades, Museum Night until dawn, and café terraces back along the Main Square.
Film-music galas at TAURON Arena, blooming Planty paths, and golden evenings on the river.
June: Wianki wreaths and fireworks beneath Wawel, the Lajkonik prancing into the square, and dragons over the Vistula.
Corpus Christi processions, long midsummer light, and Kazimierz humming with festival music.
July: Klezmer nights in Kazimierz, jazz in candlelit clubs, and riverside barge bars under warm skies.
Szeroka Street concerts, lazy boulevard afternoons, and cool salt-mine escapes from the heat.
August: Pierogi by the plateful on Mały Rynek, summer concerts on the square, and Assumption processions.
Turquoise swims at Zakrzówek, gelato on the Rynek, and warm evenings stretching past the hejnał.
September: Golden early-autumn light on the Cloth Hall, harvest flavours in the milk bars, and crisp Planty strolls.
Cooler museum days, riverside walks to Kazimierz, and the city settling into its quieter rhythm.
October: Amber leaves around the Barbican, Conrad Festival readings, and Unsound's electronic pulse after dark.
Foggy Vistula mornings, cosy café afternoons, and the first candles flickering toward All Saints.
November: Thousands of candles glowing across Rakowicki Cemetery, grey skies, and warm museum hideaways.
Independence Day flags, steaming bigos in the milk bars, and early dusk over a hushed Old Town.
December: Christmas market lights on the Rynek, szopki cribs at the Mickiewicz statue, and snow on St. Mary's towers.
Grzane wino in gloved hands, oscypek on the grill, and New Year's fireworks over the Main Square.
